p1_string_of_nickels.physical_facts: Yellow pot, dense foliage, stable turgidity.
p1_string_of_nickels.explanatory_transformations: Maintained consistent leaf density throughout the 5-day sequence; no significant wilting or drop.
p1_string_of_nickels.visual_health_reasoning: Healthy; leaves remain plump and well-aligned with the pot rim, indicating successful moisture retention.

p2_mexican_mint.physical_facts: Black pot, central growth, two primary leaves with smaller emerging pair.
p2_mexican_mint.explanatory_transformations: Showed initial signs of dehydration (drooping) in early images; recent images show stabilization following the starch water application.
p2_mexican_mint.visual_health_reasoning: Recovering; the leaf margins are less necrotic than the T-5 baseline, suggesting the starch water has improved soil-root contact.

p3_pothos.physical_facts: Black pot, two leaves, white rabbit anchor (5cm).
p3_pothos.explanatory_transformations: Leaf tip necrosis observed at T-5 has remained static; no further degradation noted.
p3_pothos.visual_health_reasoning: Stable; the necrosis is localized and not spreading, indicating the plant has reached a homeostasis despite VPD stress.

p4_silver_guest.physical_facts: Black pot (shared with p2), small sprout near rim.
p4_silver_guest.explanatory_transformations: Remains small; growth is slow but consistent with the established baseline.
p4_silver_guest.visual_health_reasoning: Stable; no signs of chlorosis or wilting.

biome_observations.soil_surface: Presence of white granular material (starch water residue) confirms recent human intervention.
biome_observations.biome_anomalies: None; soil moisture appears consistent across all pots.

temporal_deltas.T_minus_5_to_T_minus_4: Introduction of white starch residue on soil surface.
temporal_deltas.T_minus_4_to_CURRENT: Gradual stabilization of p2 leaf posture; no further degradation of p3 leaf tips.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The garden is currently in a state of recovery. The starch water application has successfully mitigated the acute dehydration observed in p2. p1 and p3 remain stable. No new stress markers identified.
vision_report.anomalies: None; all visual changes are consistent with the reported human action of adding starch water.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit confirms that the garden is responding positively to the supplementary starch water. p2, which was previously showing signs of stress, has stabilized. The white residue on the soil is a direct result of the human intervention and is not a sign of fungal or salt buildup. The plants are currently in a stable, maintenance-oriented state.
